dominant_theme: Dependency conflicts and version compatibility issues are the primary blocker for AI framework adoption and deployment.
summary: AI workflow tools face critical instability from dependency management failures, with Tenacity, httpx, and internal version conflicts causing cascading breakages. Asynchronous operations and streaming implementations remain fundamentally unreliable, particularly in production-scale scenarios. Framework-level architecture issues around structured outputs, agent design patterns, and tool execution create significant user friction. The ecosystem lacks comprehensive multi-model support and standardized abstractions, forcing developers into tool-specific implementations.

painpoints.title: Dependency version conflicts blocking deployments
painpoints.description: Critical library upgrades (Tenacity 8.4.0, httpx 0.28.0) introduce breaking changes and import errors across LangChain, LlamaIndex, and AutoGen frameworks. Dependency resolution becomes impossible without direct team intervention, creating deployment blockers and version lock-in scenarios.
sample_quotes: It's literally impossible to fix this issue without intervention from LLAMA INDEX team
Tenacity library recently updated to v8.4.0 and they had broken some of the imports

painpoints.title: Asynchronous operations timeout under load
painpoints.description: Constant timeouts occur after multiple async calls, particularly with streaming operations exceeding 5 minutes. Connection failures and InvalidChunkLength errors prevent long-form response processing, making production deployments of async pipelines unreliable.
sample_quotes: Constant timeouts after multiple asynchronous calls
Streaming output from gpt4 consistently fails with InvalidChunkLength for prompts that trigger long-form responses

painpoints.title: Structured output parsing returns wrong types
painpoints.description: LLM responses fail to parse into expected Pydantic models, returning strings instead. Advanced schema support is inconsistent across providers (OpenAI, Google Generative AI), breaking downstream validation and type safety in workflows.
sample_quotes: When using Structured Output, there are some cases that response from LLM can't be parsed into a Pydantic Model
Structured Output doesn't work with advanced schema

painpoints.title: Streaming implementation gaps and instability
painpoints.description: Streaming support is incomplete across frameworks, with requests to add streaming function calls and message streaming. Connection breaks after extended periods prevent real-time application use cases and long-running request handling.

painpoints.title: Agent framework complexity and tool execution
painpoints.description: AutoGen's heavy reliance on code generation creates friction for simple tool execution workflows. Developers need lightweight agent patterns that separate tool proposal from execution without requiring full code generation capabilities.
sample_quotes: Autogen heavily works on the concept of generating code and executing it. Does it have a simple way of executing code directly after selection?

painpoints.title: Callback and tracking mechanism failures
painpoints.description: Monitoring utilities like get_openai_callback break after framework updates, preventing cost tracking and execution monitoring. Lack of stable observability APIs complicates debugging and billing audits in production environments.
sample_quotes: get_openai_callback not working when using Agent Executor after updating to latest version

painpoints.title: Limited non-OpenAI model support and parity
painpoints.description: Frameworks prioritize OpenAI integration while non-OpenAI models (Bedrock, PaLM, open-source LLMs) lack equivalent features and reliability. Tool calling, streaming, and structured outputs work inconsistently across provider implementations.
sample_quotes: Bedrock Converse Chat with Tools is throwing error in latest release

painpoints.title: Source attribution and retrieval reliability
painpoints.description: RetrievalQA chains return unreliable sources lacking transparency into document provenance. Loss of source tracking breaks compliance requirements and prevents users from validating LLM reasoning in RAG applications.

painpoints.title: Language/runtime ecosystem fragmentation
painpoints.description: AI frameworks lack Golang and Rust implementations, limiting async capabilities and system integration. Python-only implementations create bottlenecks for high-performance and concurrent AI applications in enterprise environments.
sample_quotes: Introducing Rust and Golang implementations for Microsoft Autogen can significantly enhance its asynchronous capabilities

painpoints.title: Breaking changes in minor version releases
painpoints.description: Non-semantic versioning introduces regressions across patch/minor updates (ChatCompletionToolParam type removal, Responses API breaks). Developers cannot safely update dependencies, creating security and stability dilemmas.
sample_quotes: ChatCompletionToolParam no longer a valid type in 1.99.2
Using Responses API + Streaming seems to be broken with ImportError in 1.99
